# slurm Provider

Manage Slurm accounting resources (accounts, users, QOS) via the slurmrestd REST API.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "slurm" {
  endpoint    = "http://slurmrestd.example.com:6820"
  token       = var.slurm_token
  cluster     = "mycluster"
  api_version = "v0.0.42"
}

variable "slurm_token" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `api_version` (String) The slurmrestd API version (e.g. v0.0.42). Can also be set with the SLURM_API_VERSION environment variable. Defaults to v0.0.42 (Slurm 25.05.x).
- `cluster` (String) The Slurm cluster name. Used to scope association operations. Can also be set with the SLURM_CLUSTER environment variable.
- `endpoint` (String) The URL of the slurmrestd REST API (e.g. http://localhost:6820). Can also be set with the SLURM_REST_URL environment variable.
- `token` (String, Sensitive) JWT token for authenticating to slurmrestd. Can also be set with the SLURM_JWT_TOKEN environment variable.

## Slurm version compatibility

The provider is tested against every release below. Set `api_version` to the
value matching your cluster's Slurm release:

| Slurm release | `api_version` |
|---------------|---------------|
| 25.05.x       | `v0.0.42`     |
| 25.11.x       | `v0.0.44`     |
| 26.05.x       | `v0.0.45`     |

### Feature availability by Slurm release

All resources (`slurm_account`, `slurm_user`, `slurm_qos`) and the entity data
sources (`slurm_account`, `slurm_user`, `slurm_qos`, `slurm_partition`) work
with every supported release. Features introduced by newer Slurm releases:

| Feature | Minimum Slurm release | Minimum `api_version` |
|---------|-----------------------|-----------------------|
| `data.slurm_conf` (active slurmctld configuration) | 26.05 | `v0.0.45` |
| `data.slurm_dbd_conf` (active slurmdbd configuration) | 26.05 | `v0.0.45` |
| `data.slurm_partition` attributes `flags` and `preempt_mode` | 26.05 | `v0.0.45` |

Using the `slurm_conf`/`slurm_dbd_conf` data sources with an older
`api_version` fails at plan time with an error that names the required Slurm
release and API version. When `api_version` is new enough but the cluster
itself runs an older Slurm release, the error instead points at the server
version. Version-dependent *attributes* (like the partition `flags`) degrade
gracefully instead: they are null on API versions that do not expose them.
